title: Table-in-out buffering: example.sum_all_columns((SELECT a, b FROM range(N)))
description: Buffering table-in-out that aggregates inputs into per-column sums. Tests state management plus reduction.
setup_sql: CREATE OR REPLACE TEMP TABLE bench_input AS SELECT i::BIGINT AS a, (i*2)::BIGINT AS b FROM range(${param:rows}) t(i);

payload.schema_out: BIGINT sum_a, BIGINT sum_b — but only 1 row total, so per-input-row output is ~0
payload.notes: Reduction: N input rows -> 1 output row. Measure bytes-in/sec, not bytes-out/sec.

notes: Buffering reducer. The honest throughput metric is input bytes/sec; output is fixed at 1 row regardless of N.
